title: Euthanasia
content:
[To E. C.]


     Oh, drop your eyelids down, my lady;
        Oh, drop your eyelids down.
     'Twere well to keep your bright eyes shady
        For pity of the town!
     But should there any glances be,
     I pray you give them all to me;
     For though my life be lost thereby,
     It were the sweetest death to die!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Arthur Macy, titled "Euthanasia", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
